_comment: Offline sample mirroring BriefingResponse + BriefingResult (src/upstreamwx/api/models.py, engine/models.py). At M0.4 this fetch is swapped for POST /v1/briefing; the render layer is unchanged.
warnings: SPC convective outlook unavailable — lightning posture uses SREF + AFD only (NFR-6).
summary: Afternoon convection is the day's story. SREF puts thunderstorm probability over the upstream watershed in the elevated band, and the same-day HREF sharpens that into a High flash-flood signal for this slot — where even modest rates flood fast. Lightning is High on the exposed approach and egress. Heat reaches Extreme Caution midday; a wet party exiting near dusk faces Elevated cold. Phases were inferred from the overall window. Verify against the linked NWS sources before committing.

phases.applicable: Flash flood, Heat, Cold (no lightning — sheltered)
phases.note: Slot canyon: conservative flood fallback applied.

hazard_detail.label: High
drivers: SREF P(precip/thunder) 48% over the upstream HUC-12 domain
HREF neighborhood P(≥0.5 in/1 h) 44% — same-day high-res sharpening (FR-7a)
AFD: scattered afternoon thunderstorms, locally heavy rain
Antecedent precip in prior 24–72 h: none
hazard_detail.logic: Extreme = active Flash Flood Warning; High = Watch OR SREF ≥60% w/ convective rate, OR HREF NEP(≥0.5 in/1 h) ≥40%. Slot fallback: any meaningful slot rate → at least High (Appendix B §16.1).
assumptions: Slot canyon — conservative flood fallback applied; flagged per Appendix B §16.1.

hazard_detail.label: High
drivers: SREF P(thunderstorm) 52% in the egress window
SPC convective outlook unavailable this cycle (NFR-6)
hazard_detail.logic: High = SREF P(tstm) 40–69% OR SPC Slight/Enhanced during an exposed phase (Appendix B §16.2). Excluded from the technical span — sheltered in-slot (FR-14c).
assumptions: Lightning applies on approach and egress only; the technical span is treated as sheltered (FR-14c).

hazard_detail.label: Extreme Caution
drivers: Heat index 95 °F midday (NWS Extreme Caution band, 90–103 °F)
Approach exertion under load — effective strain runs one category hotter
hazard_detail.logic: Uses NWS Heat Index categories directly (FR-15): Caution 80–90 °F, Extreme Caution 90–103 °F, Danger 103–124 °F, Extreme Danger ≥125 °F.
assumptions: Approach advisory: effective heat strain runs one category hotter under load (Appendix B §16.3).

hazard_detail.label: Elevated
drivers: Wind 12 mph, gusts 22 — folded into apparent temperature
hazard_detail.logic: Elevated = apparent temp 46–60 °F for a wet party — the 'deceptively mild' band (Appendix B §16.4). Bands are warmer than dry-cold thresholds because wet clothing loses insulation.
assumptions: Posture assumes the party exits wet (FR-16); discount ~one tier if the activity stays dry.
